stories.headline: Mindanao quake death toll rises to 45 as recovery and power restoration continue
stories.summary: The NDRRMC said deaths from the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck off Sarangani on June 8 reached 45 as of the evening of June 9, with 33 fatalities recorded in Soccsksargen and 12 in the Davao Region, alongside 487 injured and 17 still missing. The agency said about 71,525 consumers remained without power, down from a peak of 130,463. PHIVOLCS recorded 2,023 aftershocks as of Wednesday morning, 47 of them felt by residents.
stories.context: The magnitude 7.8 offshore quake on June 8 was among the strongest to hit Mindanao in decades and triggered tsunami warnings that were later lifted.
stories.watch_for: PHIVOLCS warned that strong aftershocks remain possible in the coming days; Energy Secretary Sharon Garin said power supply is expected to return to normal within the week.

stories.headline: Marcos pledges P50,000 per family of quake fatalities, P100 million for General Santos
stories.summary: During a visit to General Santos City on June 10, President Marcos said the families of those killed in the Sarangani earthquake will each receive P50,000 in government assistance. He also announced P100 million from the Local Government Support Fund to repair the city's damaged city hall, and said the budget department would release further funds for infrastructure repairs in Sarangani and South Cotabato. Marcos said power had been restored to about 85 percent of affected areas.

stories.headline: Grid operator says quake-damaged Mindanao transmission lines fully restored
stories.summary: The National Grid Corporation of the Philippines said it completed restoration of the transmission lines toppled by the magnitude 7.8 earthquake, with services in South Cotabato, Sarangani, and General Santos City back online at 1:38 p.m. on June 9. The Department of Energy directed the grid operator to speed up assessment and repairs, while damage evaluation continued across generation and distribution facilities.

stories.headline: Fuel prices climb anew, with diesel up as much as P5.77 per liter
stories.summary: Oil companies raised pump prices effective June 9, with gasoline up by P0.30 per liter across firms and diesel increasing by P4.85 to P5.77 depending on the retailer, according to advisories from Seaoil, Shell, and Unioil. Kerosene rose by up to P8.10 per liter. An industry source attributed the increases to supply disruption following stalled peace negotiations in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Gatchalian rejects power-sharing as Senate leadership dispute with Cayetano persists
stories.summary: Acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian rejected proposals for a power-sharing arrangement to resolve the chamber's leadership standoff, saying only one person was elected to lead when the new majority installed him on June 3. Senator Alan Peter Cayetano, who was unseated in that session after his bloc boycotted it, maintains he remains the legitimate Senate President and has proposed interim dual-signatory arrangements. Malacanang and the House of Representatives recognize Gatchalian.

stories.headline: Senate impeachment court sets pre-trial conference in VP Duterte case
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court, through presiding officer Sherwin Gatchalian, issued a notice scheduling pre-trial proceedings in the impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte. The pre-trial will cover stipulation of facts, marking of evidence, identification of witnesses, and the setting of trial dates. Duterte faces charges including alleged misuse of confidential funds, unexplained wealth, and statements against President Marcos and other officials.
stories.watch_for: The pre-trial conference is set for June 18 at the Senate, with both parties required to submit pre-trial briefs by June 15. The proceedings will be closed to the media and the public.

stories.headline: Unemployment rate eased to 4.7 percent in April; Palace urges continued job creation
stories.summary: Official data showed the unemployment rate fell to 4.7 percent in April from 5.8 percent in March, with the number of employed Filipinos rising to 48.89 million and the services sector accounting for 62.3 percent of jobs. Palace Press Officer Claire Castro said the results were positive but that the government would not take them for granted and would continue working to create quality jobs and livelihood programs.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings rains to northern Luzon; isolated thunderstorms elsewhere
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon is bringing cloudy skies with scattered rains and thunderstorms over Ilocos Norte, Batanes, and the Babuyan Islands, with possible flash floods and landslides in affected areas. The rest of the country can expect partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers and thunderstorms, mainly in the afternoon and evening.

stories.headline: Two Ateneo basketball players drown during team-building activity in Aurora
stories.summary: Two members of the Ateneo de Manila men's basketball team, 19-year-old incoming recruit Rene Baterbonia and 21-year-old foreign student-athlete Divine Adili, died in a drowning incident during a team-building activity in Dipaculao, Aurora, on June 8. Police initial findings attributed Baterbonia's death to asphyxia by drowning, and authorities said they were still investigating the circumstances. The university expressed condolences to the players' families.
